title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1985, American jazz bassist and composer Charlie Haden brings his own New Liberation Orchestra to the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague for a performance.

title: Chicken
subTitle: The audience at the Montreal Jazz Festival in Canada was in for a treat on July 3, 1982, as a musical innovator hit the stage; Jaco Pastorius transforms the electric bass into a solo instrument.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: The acclaimed jazz vocalist, Melody Gardot, enchants us at the Chateau d'Hérouville in France.

title: European Tour: Live in '64, '65 & '70 - Bill Evans, Chuck Israels & Larry Bunker
subTitle: Bill Evans: Live in '64, '65 & '70 features five separate performances by one of the most revered pianists in music history.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: In 2019, the city of Sofia, Bulgaria, hosts the International A to JazZ Festival; among the featured acts is the jazz trumpeter Christian Scott aTunde Adjuah.

title: Live in Palais des Beaux-Arts - Thelonious Monk, Charlie Rouse, Frankie Dunlop & John Ore
subTitle: Thelonious Monk performs live in Belgium in the winter of 1965.

title: Live in Belgium - Thelonious Monk, Charlie Rouse, John Ore & Frankie Dunlop
subTitle: Thelonious Monk: Live in '65 features an intimate concert filmed three days apart in Belgium in the Winter of 1965 with a legendary quartet that includes drummer Frankie Dunlop, bassist John Ore and tenor saxophonist Charlie Rouse.

title: Song for my Father
subTitle: Horace Silver created a jazz classic honoring his father. The piece has a catchy tune with bossa nova feel and expresses deep emotion.

title: Michel Legrand Orchestra: Spa, 1982
subTitle: Michel Legrand and his orchestra perform a number of his soundtrack compositions during a spirited 1982 performance in the Belgian town of Spa.

subTitle: This selection of performances by some of the most influential jazz guitarists in history showcases their unique essence.

title: Adjustment
subTitle: Jazz pianist Horace Silver's fusion of hard bop, blues, soulful grooves and Cape Verdean influences have left a mark on the world of jazz.

title: Dee Dee Bridgewater: Motherland
subTitle: With 'Red Earth', American singer Dee Dee Bridgewater goes on a Malian journey that merges & melds all borders. She brings her own experiences as an African-American woman back to the motherland, resulting in a fusion of feeling, visions, and music.

title: Raul Midon at Singapore 2017
subTitle: Raul Midón appears at 2017's Sing Jazz Festival in Singapore with double bassist Romeir Mendez and drummer Billy Williams.

title: Jazzed Out Oslo
subTitle: The singular quality of the Jazzed Out collection comes out particularly clearly in Oslo.

title: Estival Jazz Lugano
subTitle: Pianist and jazz icon Sun Ra and his crew perform at the Estival Jazz festival in Lugano, Switzerland; Sun Ra Arkestra performs a concert.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: Buddy Rich, an influential jazz drummer, performed at the North Sea Jazz Festival of 1978 with his orchestra; Rich's drumming was known for its remarkable speed and dexterity, which he achieved without formal education!

title: Charles Mingus and Eric Dolphy Live in Liège
subTitle: Charles Mingus showcases a concert performed in April 1965 featuring Jaki Byard on piano, Dannie Richmond on drums, Johnny Coles on trumpet, Clifford Jordan on tenor sax and Eric Dolphy on alto sax, flute and bass clarinet.

title: Lianne La Havas: Live at Casino de Paris
subTitle: Lianne La Havas' performance at Casino de Paris shows the British singer-songwriter at her best; this concert is part of La Havas' "Blood Tour", promoting her second album "Blood."

title: John Lee Hooker Live in Paris
subTitle: American blues singer and guitarist John Lee Hooker is joined by Carey Bell on harmonica, Joe Harper on bass, Lester Dorsie on drums and Jimmy Dawkins on electric guitar in this 1970 performance from Maison de la Radio in Paris, France.

title: Modern Jazz Quartet at the Bozar
subTitle: The Modern Jazz Quartet (MJQ) was composed of Milt 'Bags' Jackson on vibraphone, John Lewis (piano), Percy Heath (bass), and Connie Kay (drums). Jackson and Lewis were pioneer bebop musicians who had played together in Dizzy Gillespie's big band.

title: Stan Getz: The Last Recording
subTitle: Regarded as the greatest instrumental soloist of all-time, Stanley Gayetsky, famously known as Stan Getz, emerges as one of the most significant musical forces in the world of jazz post World War II.

subTitle: Thelonious Monk performs live in Belgium in the winter of 1965.

subTitle: This selection of performances by some of the most influential jazz guitarists in history showcases their unique essence.

title: James Brown : The North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1981, the legendary American singer James Brown gave an unforgettable performance to the audience at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague, the Netherlands.

title: Monty Alexander Quartet -- North Sea Jazz
subTitle: On July 13, 1979, Jamaican pianist Monty Alexander was joined by bassist Steve Rodby, Frank Gant, Bobby Thomas and Othello Molineaux at the "North Sea Jazz Festival" in The Hague.

title: Jazz Open Stuttgart: West Coast All Stars
subTitle: Celebrating 50 years of West Coast Jazz, a cool form of jazz played mostly by studio musicians as a break from their studio work; showing early pioneers like Conte Candoli, Carl Fontana and Teddy Edwards.

title: Live in Australia - Louis Armstrong, Billy Kyle, Trummy Young & Aervell Shaw
subTitle: In 1964 Louis Armstrong recorded his biggest-selling record, Hello, Dolly! He made assorted television appearances, especially in the 1950s and 1960s - the recorded film was a TV Show in Australia when Armstrong was at the peak of his career.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: Embark on a journey through the world of jazz piano as we showcase some iconic and influential pianists in history; this collection of performances captures the unique essence and brilliance of legendary pianists.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: Features a concert from Salif Keita, a talented musician who combines Malian, European and American influences by using a range of both West African and Western European musical instruments.

title: Münchner Klaviersommer: Weather Update
subTitle: Jazz-fusion band Weather Update, founded by Austrian keyboardist Joe Zawinul, performs at Münchner Klaviersommer, in Munich, Germany in 1986.

title: Jazzmeia Horn at Malta Jazz Festival
subTitle: Vocalist Jazzmeia Horn's 2019 Malta Jazz Festival appearance; a celebration of jazz's future, infused with the soul of its past.

title: Estival Jazz Lugano
subTitle: In 1986, American pianist and composer Keith Jarrett is joined by bassist Gary Peacock and drummer Jack DeJohnette at Estival Jazz Lugano; known as the Standards Trio, they put on an enthralling performance.

title: Christian McBride Quartet at Malta 2018
subTitle: Double bassist Christian McBride presents his band New Jawn at the Malta Jazz Festival on July 20, 2018; hailing from Philadelphia, this musician combines jazz, R&B, pop/rock, hip hop/neo-soul, and classical.

title: Jazz à Ramatuelle
subTitle: Italian jazz pianist Antonio Faraò.

subTitle: The Art Farmer concert from 1964 featuring the great flugelhornist in his prime.

title: Münchner Klaviersommer: Lionel Hampton
subTitle: At Münchner Klaviersommer 1994, American swing band leader Lionel Hampton performs jazz standards with Junior Mance on piano, Jimmy Woode on bass and Bobby Durham on drums; they are joined by the St. Petersburg State Orchestra on two numbers.

subTitle: Dino Saluzzi, Charlie Mariano and Wolfgang Dauner perform live in Stuttgart.

title: Concertgebouw Amsterdam 1967 -- Sammy Davis Jr
subTitle: In 1967, American singer and actor Sammy Davis Jr. appeared at the Concertgebouw in Amsterdam for a live, nationwide TV broadcast; supported by his band, Davis combined his singing talent and sense of humor as he covered songs.

title: Omer Avital -- Jazz in Duketown
subTitle: Israeli-American double bassist Omer Avital plays radiantly energetic, resounding jazz with oriental influences; at Jazz in Duketown 2019, Avital presents singular repertoire based on his album "Qantar."

title: Omer Avital -- Jazz in Duketown
subTitle: Israeli-American double bassist Omer Avital plays radiantly energetic, resounding jazz with oriental influences; at Jazz in Duketown 2019, Avital presents singular repertoire based on his album "Qantar."

title: Aretha Franklin in Paris
subTitle: Recording at the Palais Des Sports from 1977; performing songs like "Respect," "You Make Me Feel Like a Natural Woman," "La Vie en Rose" and "Singing in the Rain."

subTitle: In 1964 Louis Armstrong recorded his biggest-selling record, Hello, Dolly! He made assorted television appearances, especially in the 1950s and 1960s - the recorded film was a TV Show in Australia when Armstrong was at the peak of his career.

title: Lady Day: The Many Faces of Billie Holiday
subTitle: Film clips show the jazz/blues artist performing "God Bless the Child" and "Please Don't Talk About Me When I'm Gone."

title: Mack the knife
subTitle: This selection of unforgettable performances by some of the most iconic and influential male jazz and pop vocalists in history showcases these legends' unique essence.

title: Estival Jazz Lugano
subTitle: In 1996, renowned American saxophonist Wayne Shorter and his quintet perform at Estival Jazz Lugano; together with pianist Jim Beard, guitarist David Gilmore, bassist Alphonso Johnson and drummer Rodney van Holmes.

title: Vigil
subTitle: Explore the music of an influential and innovative saxophonist in jazz: John Coltrane; get immersed in this collection of performances from the early 1960s that showcase Coltrane's artistry.

subTitle: John Lewis Piano Solo

title: Miles Davis: Jazz Greats

subTitle: Raul Midón appeared at 2017's Sing Jazz Festival in Singapore with double bassist Romeir Mendez and drummer Billy Williams.

title: Münchner Klaviersommer: Eliane Elias Trio
subTitle: In this live concert at the Philharmonie in Munich, Eliane Elias shows her distinctive and immediately recognizable musical style, which blends her Brazilian samba roots and voice with her instrumental bebop, jazz and compositional skills.

title: Dizzy Gillespie - Jazz Greats
subTitle: American jazz trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie was an influential figure of bebop known for fusing Cuban, Latin American, and Carribean influences in his music.

subTitle: November Music, a Dutch music festival, showcases prominent composers, ensembles and pioneers of jazz and world music; Armenian pianist Hamasyan; Norwegian trumpeter Mathias Eick.
